title: Hydrangea 'Hot Chocolate'. 9cm Plant x 2. Summer Flowering Shrub. Purple Blue Flowers. Available from Bedwen Plants
features: Hydrangea 'Hot Chocolate' is a handsome small to medium sized, deciduous shrub with large, soft chocolate burgundy coloured leaves and violet-blue lacecap-like flowers in mid to late summer.
The unusual leaves offer a second surprise in autumn as they fade to deep green before turning to amber and golden shades. This robust, hardy shrub will flower right through summer with minimal care.
Grow in partial shade in any moist but well-drained soil. If grown in full sun ensure the soil remains moist. Improve soils with organic matter
description: Product Description About Us The nursery was started by Darren Shipp in 2016. Working at his family winery for over 10 years, Darren gained a keen interest in the food industry and first-hand experience in running a business. After the winery relocated to a new site in a village called Henllan, Darren decided to turn his long-standing interest in growing edibles into a fully-fledged business. As the business focused on growing plants for the kitchen garden, it began under the name Ediplants. In 2020, after successfully expanding the nursery to now supply a large selection of ornamental as well as edible plants, Darren decided to change the name to Bedwen Plants. Our larger plant sizes (Sizes refer to rootstock. Actual plant size is dependent on species, variety, and the time of year of purchase) 7cm Plant 7cm refers to the diameter of the pot commonly used in plant retail. The size of the plant can vary greatly. Most will be between 10-30cm tall unless they are naturally low growing plants or if you are buying herbaceous plants in winter. Size approx: 6x7cm by 6cm deep. 9cm Plant 9cm refers to the diameter of the pot commonly used in plant retail. The size of the plant can vary greatly. Most will be between 10-30cm tall unless they are naturally low growing plants or if you are buying herbaceous plants in winter. Size approx: 9cm by 10cm deep. 1L Plant 1L refers to the volume of the pot, the roots are almost double the size of a 9cm pot. The size of the plant can vary greatly, most will be between 20-50cm tall unless they are naturally low growing plants or if you are buying herbaceous plants in winter. Size approx: 13xcm by 11cm deep. 2L Plant 2L refers to the volume of the pot, the roots are almost 4 times the size of a 9cm pot. The size of the plant can vary greatly, most will be between 30-60cm tall unless they are naturally low growing plants or if you are buying herbaceous plants in winter. Size approx: 14cm x 14cm deep. Plant Types (see bullet points above) Annual/Biennial An annual plant is a plant that completes its life cycle, from germination to the production of seeds, within one growing season, and then dies. Biennials will grow in their first season and set seed in their second. Herbaceous Perennial Herbaceous plants - especially small flowering plants -grow and bloom over the spring and summer, die back every autumn and winter, and then return in the spring from their rootstock. Deciduous Deciduous plants are those that lose some or all of their leaves for part of the year, usually in the autumn/early winter, with leaf growth returning in the spring. Evergreen An evergreen is a plant which has foliage that remains green and functional through more than one growing season. These plants provide year-round colour and interest. Bedwen Plants and the Environment. Plastics We are proud to have developed a system to pack orders with reduced plastic using a combination of paper towels and bags that degrade. The boxes we use are designed to fold and hold plants in place without the need for any extra packing material and are also fully able to degrade. Pest Control At the Nursery, we are committed to only using natural methods of pest control and we monitor our plants daily so we can quickly react to any major pest issues. Our methods include spraying aromas such as garlic to deter pests or simply manual removal. While this is not the most cost-effective way, we believe it is essential for the environment. Peat While some of the plants which we order contain peat, we are proud to be completely peat-free in all our in-home production at the nursery. Peat is formed in bog lands over many years. However, the rate at which it is being used commercially and in the home garden has damaged natural ecosystems considerably.
